steps.title: Show the resulting shift
steps.content: The first sentence says Barad's theory rebukes a long-standing dualistic divide in scientific thought. The next sentence says the theory presents a paradigmatic shift. The transition must show a logical result or consequence.

steps.title: Reject example and contrast cues
steps.content: A would introduce an example, but the second sentence draws a conclusion. B and D signal contrast or qualification, while the second sentence reinforces the importance of the first. The needed transition means "therefore" in this context. The answer is C.
